mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate, directly lifting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 5.2% within a single month, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger recession fears, compressing equity multiples and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises above 5.2% in a single month
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day forward returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment threshold breach.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous contraction in industrial production and a surge in consumer credit balances indicates a supply-demand mismatch where demand is shifting from goods to services, reducing corporate earnings expectations and depressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of out-of-sample periods where both proxies move in the specified directions.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation environment where CPI exceeds 195.0, an increase in the federal funds rate accelerates real borrowing costs, squeezing corporate profit margins and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL > 195.0 and FEDFUNDS rises > 0.25% in a month
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with sticky inflation
failure_condition: SPX forward returns do not decline significantly when both CPI and FEDFUNDS rise simultaneously under the specified threshold.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained month-over-month increase in housing starts reflects rising business and consumer confidence, which translates into higher aggregate demand and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns in rising housing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When nonfarm payrolls fall below 134,000 thousand persons, it signals a sharp deceleration in economic activity that precedes earnings downgrades, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day forward returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite the payroll threshold breach.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where real GDP growth slows while consumer credit balances remain elevated indicates unsustainable consumption funded by debt, which will eventually force a demand correction and depress S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly growth < 1.5% while RSAFS rises > 2.0% MoM
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of out-of-sample periods where GDP growth slows but credit expands.

mechanism_narrative: During a supply-constrained environment where industrial production exceeds 109.0, a simultaneous rise in the unemployment rate indicates structural labor shortages that will force wage-price spirals, prompting aggressive monetary tightening and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO > 109.0 and UNRATE rises > 0.2pp in a month
failure_condition: SPX forward returns do not decline significantly when both industrial production and unemployment rise simultaneously under the specified threshold.
